one_line: Elder zamindar-warrior of Jagdispur leading the 1857 revolt in Bihar.

appearance.age_look: Looks around 65–70, lean but wiry, with the posture of a seasoned fighter despite age.
appearance.face: Oval, weathered brown complexion with deep lines around eyes and mouth; sharp aquiline nose, full grey mustache, neatly trimmed short grey beard along jaw; thick arched brows; right forearm often subtly bandaged; default expression calm, steely and resolute with a hint of stern warmth.
appearance.hair: Long, wavy hair mostly grey with a few darker strands, tied back in a low knot; covered by a simple off‑white cotton pagri (turban) wrapped flat and close to the head, with a narrow dark maroon band near the base and no ornament.
appearance.costume: Era-accurate mid‑19th‑century north Indian zamindar-warrior attire: a knee-length off‑white cotton angarkha (overlapping wrap tunic) with a slight flare at the hem and side‑ties, worn over a light beige kurta; a wide dark maroon cotton patka (sash) tightly wrapped around the waist with one end tucked, hinting at a hidden pouch; straight, slightly loose white churidar trousers gathered at the ankles; sturdy dark brown leather mojari shoes with modest upturned toes; over the angarkha, a sleeveless quilted cotton coat (nimak) in muted olive green with subtle vertical stitching, fitted closely to the torso to suggest light protection; minimal jewellery—small simple gold stud in each ear and a plain iron bangle on the left wrist; a narrow dark maroon scarf occasionally draped around shoulders but kept tidy, giving him a disciplined zamindar-commander look.
appearance.signature: A slightly curved talwar with a plain steel blade, brass hilt and disc pommel, carried in a dark brown leather scabbard worn at his left side on a simple brown leather belt; his right forearm is wrapped in off‑white cloth bandage, hinting at an old wound but kept functional.

ref_desc: Elder Kunwar Singh has an oval weathered brown face with grey mustache and beard, long grey hair in a low knot under an off‑white turban, wearing an off‑white angarkha, olive quilted sleeveless coat, white churidar, dark maroon sash, brown mojari, right forearm bandage, and a curved talwar in a dark brown scabbard at his left side with off‑white, dark maroon, olive green, dark brown and beige colours.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Kunwar Singh as a dignified elder zamindar-warrior from mid-19th-century Bihar: an oval, weathered brown face with deep lines, sharp nose, thick arched brows, a full grey mustache and a neatly trimmed short grey beard along the jaw, his expression calm and steely; long, wavy grey hair tied in a low knot and covered by a simple off‑white cotton turban wrapped flat and close to the head with a narrow dark maroon band at the base and no ornament. Show him in a knee-length off‑white cotton angarkha with overlapping front panels and side ties over a light beige kurta, a wide dark maroon cotton sash tightly wrapped at the waist, and straight slightly loose white churidar trousers gathered at the ankles. Over the angarkha he wears a sleeveless quilted cotton coat in muted olive green with visible vertical stitching, closely fitted to the torso, and on his feet sturdy dark brown leather mojari shoes with modest upturned toes. Add minimal jewellery: small plain gold studs in both ears and a single plain iron bangle on his left wrist. His right forearm is wrapped in an off‑white cloth bandage, suggesting an old wound but still usable. At his left side, on a simple brown leather belt, hangs his signature weapon: a slightly curved talwar with a plain steel blade, brass hilt and disc pommel in a dark brown leather scabbard. Keep the dominant colours off‑white, dark maroon, olive green, dark brown and beige, and present him front, side and three-quarter views in consistent anime proportions while keeping all historical clothing and weapon details clear and identical.
